title: crepis coronopus
intro: Crepis coronopus, also known as Rough Hawk's-beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its jagged, hairy leaves and small yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Crepis coronopus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
